items.title: Inside this town, 'it's not a secret' that half the people don't officially exist

children.content: In a community like no other, what happens when people aren't counted? Welcome to the supposedly small country town with hidden houses and five Asian supermarkets.

items.title: Another group of Australians with IS links leave camp in Syria for Australia

image.altText: A bus driving down a road in the middle of a flat plain.

children.content: The ABC has seen buses carrying the group, which left the al-Roj camp on Thursday afternoon, local time.

items.title: Inside the industrial-scale slave system run by Islamic State

children.content: Australia is the latest country to prosecute its own citizens over Islamic State's system of Yazidi slavery, part of a growing global reckoning over the role some foreign women allegedly played in the atrocities.

image.altText: Two veiled women walk past a billboard that urges women to wear a hijab

items.title: Eleven Australian flotilla activists released from Israeli detention

children.content: Social Services Minister Tanya Plibersek has told ABC News Breakfast that the 11 Australians are now in Istanbul and are being assisted by Australian officials.

image.altText: Activists from the Gaza-bound Global Sumud Flotilla walk off an airplane in turkiye

items.title: What we know about Iran's new system of transit in the Strait of Hormuz

children.content: Iran is asserting unprecedented control over the strategically critical waterway, with ships reportedly facing screening processes, transit approvals and potential Bitcoin-backed insurance requirements.

image.altText: A drone view shows the Malta-flagged tanker Agios Fanourios I arriving in Iraq's territorial waters off Basra.

items.title: Republican revolt over Trump 'weaponisation' fund stalls ICE funding vote

children.content: US Senate Republicans have furious internal disagreements over a proposed compensation fund for Trump allies who claim they have been unfairly targeted by federal agencies.

image.altText: Donald Trump speaking in the aisle on board Air Force One.

items.title: Cuba on the brink: Residents describe life 'trapped between two psychopaths'

children.content: As power and petrol supplies run out, Cubans tell the ABC about their daily lives. "You can no longer find streets packed with travellers, music and dance," one says. "Only Cuban nationals, still attempting to take some bread home."

articles.title: Cuba accepts offer of $140m in American aid, Rubio says

children.content: Tensions between the longtime Cold War rivals have hit a new low in recent days after the US indicted the country's 94-year-old former president Raúl Castro.

image.altText: Marco Rubio speaking to reporters outside.

items.title: Eleven Australian activists detained by Israel now in Istanbul, minister says

children.content: Social Services Minister Tanya Plibersek says 11 Australian activists who were aboard a flotilla to Gaza and were detained by Israel are in Türkiye. Follow live.

items.title: ASX to climb, oil drops on US–Iran deal hopes

children.content: Australian markets are set to rise this morning, as oil falls on hopes of a diplomatic way out of the war in Iran. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Snowy bosses paid $1.2m in bonuses as hydro project costs spiral

children.content: The renewables project's chief executive, Dennis Barnes, received $323,000 in bonus pay on top of his $1.69 million salary last financial year, while four other senior executives received about $919,000 in "variable pay".

image.altText: A man wearing a construction outfit, speaking at a lectern.

items.title: There are 40,000 trusts linked to Australian farms. Here's why

children.content: Despite winning a carve-out to changes to family trusts announced in the budget, farmers are still worried about what tax reform means for them.

image.altText: people working in a field

items.title: 'Is that a UFO?' Suspected meteor lights up skies across NSW

children.content: Colourful streaks across the skies have been spotted from Bondi to Canberra as a "fireball" meteor passed through the atmosphere overnight.

image.altText: A golfer at a driving range lines up a shot as a meteor lights up the sky.

gallery.altText: Dashcam footage showing a flashing appearing across the sky

items.title: Parents of children in foster care say 'black eyes' and bite marks were ignored

children.content: The parents of two children in Northern Territory foster care have spoken out about their "heartbreaking" experience with the system, saying concerns their daughter was being abused by her foster family were brushed off by the department.

image.altText: A woman in a sleeveless, long dress, long brown hair in kitchen, blurred background, with brown teddy bear focus in foreground.

items.title: In 1990 there were 599 million mental disorder cases globally. Now there are 1.2 billion

children.content: A surge over the last three decades means mental disorders now account for more than 17 per cent of all disability worldwide, according to a report published this week.

image.altText: illustration of a woman's face cut into slides on an orange background

items.title: Why the mushroom murders still puzzle authors like Helen Garner

children.content: In May 2025, Helen Garner, Chloe Hooper and Sarah Krasnostein, three literary true-crime writers, headed to Erin Patterson's trial. The resulting book just won an Australian Book Industry Award.

gallery.altText: Sarah Krasnostein, 48, and Chloe Hooper, 52, sit on either side of a table. Helen Garner, 83, stands between them.

items.title: The hidden codes and Aussie influence in Harry Connick Jr's new symphony

children.content: Harry Connick Jr's new symphony, Elaboratio, is filled with musical tributes to his mother Anita. Orchestra Victoria and Australian conductor Jessica Gethin are bringing those memories to life at Carnegie Hall.

image.altText: A man in a black jacket with an earpiece holds a microphone with a red and purple background

items.title: Friday news quiz: A pumped prince, a grinning sculpture and a stowaway frog

children.content: You'll be as happy as Prince William when you take this week's quiz, which goes off like a frog in a
bag of lettuce.

image.altText: Kylie Minogue sings into a microphone while wearing a rainbow-coloured dress.

items.title: Sydney council to rollout fees to use dozens of public parks

children.content: A picnic vendor told the ABC they found out about the proposed fees through a group chat with other small businesses, and fears the proposed fee structure could "kill an entire industry".

gallery.altText: A woman sits on a picnic rug with a view of Sydney Harbour behind her.

thumbnail.altText: A woman sits on a picnic rug with a view of Sydney Harbour behind her.

items.title: Chalmers asked to explain how he 'landed on 30 per cent' tax rate

children.content: The Productivity Commission was not asked to model how changes to capital gains tax would impact Australia's economy. Business groups say Treasurer Jim Chalmers must explain how the government concluded its tax changes would ultimately be a boon to productivity.

image.altText: Jim Chalmers speaks to reporters.

items.title: ASIO cut counterterrorism funding share to post-9/11 low before Bondi

children.content: Australia's intelligence agencies had cut counterterrorism funding to its lowest share this century before the Bondi massacre, despite warnings that a terrorist attack was probable and that antisemitic violence posed an escalating threat to life.

image.altText: ASIO director-general Mike Burgess delivering a speech.

items.title: NT Chief Minister dismisses concerns about child protection changes as 'hysteria'

children.content: Northern Territory Chief Minister Lia Finocchiaro has labelled concerns about proposed child protection changes as "hysteria", a day after the prime minister called for "respectful" consultation with outraged Aboriginal groups.

image.altText: A side profile of a woman facing a microphone

items.title: One Nation surfs wave as Labor defends budget

children.content: Before the budget, Anthony Albanese and Jim Chalmers knew they would be hit by criticism for its broken promises, but they underestimated the extent and ferocity of the backlash.

image.altText: A man in an Akubara wraps his arms around a woman with a microphone

children.content: A new study into dingo DNA has found many animals previously considered to be purebred actually have domestic dog genetics.

items.title: Soft plastics industry at breaking point, as waste instead sent overseas

children.content: The soft plastic recycling industry wants more waste to stay here, saying too much material is being sent overseas.

image.altText: A white haired man with glasses, a blue collared shirt and bright yellow vest staring at piles of plastic while holding a piece.

items.title: Early lung damage in kids with CF linked with immune cell changes

children.content: Australian researchers have mapped how lung damage may begin early in life for children with cystic fibrosis, providing new insights that may help reshape future care.

image.altText: A boy does a lung test in a room.

items.title: US-bound plane diverted after passenger from Ebola-hit Congo boards 'in error'

children.content: The US has introduced new restrictions on travellers from countries exposed to an Ebola outbreak in Africa, requiring them to enter through Washington DC.

items.title: What is 'pink-slime' journalism and has it infiltrated Australian media?

children.content: Experts say a series of AI-generated digital news outlets is the first recorded example of "pink-slime" journalism in Australia, with the website owner claiming the now-deleted outlets were an experiment gone wrong.

items.title: AI-generated resumes making hiring 'incredibly difficult', recruiters say

children.content: AI-perfected CVs are becoming the norm as more job applicants and employers use artificial intelligence, but recruiters say the technology could be making hiring trickier.

image.altText: A close up of text on a computer screen over a woman's shoulder.

items.title: 'Absolute shock': NASCAR champion Kyle Busch dies aged 41

children.content: Kyle Busch, who won two Cup Series championships and will be a future NASCAR Hall of Famer, has died aged 41.

image.altText: A man in racing clothing smiling.

items.title: Arsenal coach cooked a barbecue instead of watching title-deciding game

children.content: Mikel Arteta says he decided to not watch the match that secured Arsenal an historic EPL title, instead deciding to cook a barbecue.

items.title: What you might not know about Pendlebury's lauded basketball background

children.content: Scott Pendlebury is about to set the record for most AFL matches played. This is the latest milestone in a stellar career that almost never was.

image.altText: A sepia photo of two boys wearing medals

items.title: Caravan repairers battling to stay afloat as travellers stay close to home

children.content: Normally at this time of year, RVs are hitting the road to head north and escape winter. But the ongoing fuel crisis is forcing travellers to stay closer to home, causing a serious economic downturn for many of the 2,500 businesses in the RV repair industry.

image.altText: RVs at a caravan park

items.title: Millions expected to attend Vivid Sydney as drone shows return

children.content: Dazzling drone shows will return to Sydney's largest art and culture festival for the first time since 2024.
